source: Invest Like the Best (YouTube) — Watts and wafers
summary: Nvidia will keep open-source models a certain time frame behind the frontier as a strategic decision, and Jensen could reach near-frontier model quality with Nvidia's own model whenever he chooses
summary_extended: Baker argues that Jensen Huang (Nvidia) is making a deliberate strategic decision to keep open-source AI models a certain time frame behind the frontier, and that Nvidia has the capability to release a near-frontier model whenever it chooses but refrains from doing so to preserve the broader AI ecosystem. The reasoning is that OpenAI and Anthropic are attempting to commoditize Nvidia's complement — i.e., make the model layer a commodity to reduce Nvidia's pricing power — and Nvidia's counter-move is to ensure open-source models remain competitive enough to fragment the closed-model market without fully displacing frontier models. Baker also notes that open-source tokens are not truly free, as they require energy and GPU compute, and that open-source model companies almost always pay Nvidia a revenue share through cloud or direct hardware purchases. The investment implication is that Nvidia retains structural leverage over the entire AI stack regardless of whether open-source or closed models win, because both paths run on Nvidia hardware, and Jensen's optionality to release a frontier model is itself a strategic deterrent that protects Nvidia's ecosystem position.
detail: Baker believes Jensen has the capability to release a near-frontier model but strategically chooses not to, in order to preserve the ecosystem. He frames this as the logical counter-move to OpenAI/Anthropic trying to commoditize Nvidia's complement. He also notes that open-source tokens are not free — they cost energy and GPU time, and open-source model companies almost always get a revenue share.
confidence_evidence: Baker presents this as his belief/inference about Jensen's strategic thinking rather than confirmed information
quote_fragment: Jensen is going to keep open source a certain time frame behind the frontier

ticker_audit.reasoning: The thesis is specifically about Nvidia's strategic decision-making around open-source AI models and Jensen Huang's optionality to release a near-frontier model — Nvidia is the central subject.

Meta is the primary publisher of leading open-source models (Llama series) and is directly implicated in the open-source ecosystem dynamic Baker describes. If Nvidia strategically keeps open-source behind the frontier, Meta's open-source model strategy is directly affected, making Meta a direct beneficiary or subject of this competitive dynamic.
